channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Redbox owner’s bankruptcy filing is the final nail in the coffin for DVDs
item.description: Those red DVD kiosks peppering Walgreens across the country may have reached their end. Last week, media company Chicken Soup for the Soul Entertainment, which owns Redbox, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Apple Redefines Legacy iPhone X and Original AirPods as 'Vintage' Technology
item.description: As part of its ongoing effort to phase out outdated products, Apple has reclassified the iconic iPhone X and original AirPods, along with HomePod, as vintage technology, marking a significant step towards their eventual obsolescence.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: EU says Meta’s paid ad-free option breaks its digital competition rules
item.description: European Union regulators accused social media company Meta Platforms on Monday of breaching the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Forget streaming subscriptions, Tubi is launching in the UK with 20,000 free movies and shows
item.description: Ad-supported streaming giant Tubi is launching in the UK, giving access to over 20,000 movies and TV episodes on-demand.
item.dc:creator: lucy.buglass@futurenet.com (Lucy Buglass) , TechRadar

item.title: Healthcare Embraces Generative AI Amid Adoption Challenges
item.description: The healthcare industry is rapidly adopting generative AI, leveraging its applications in hospital operations, administrative tasks, clinical trials, and drug discovery to drive efficiency and innovation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenSSH vulnerability regreSSHion puts millions of servers at risk
item.description: Researchers have uncovered a serious remote code execution vulnerability in the Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH) server that could let unauthenticated attackers obtain a root shell on servers and take them over.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Error in new lung transplant algorithm harmed sick and dying patients
item.description: A flawed new algorithm for distributing lungs to transplant patients wound up putting people with Type O blood at a disadvantage.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Royal Pratt, The Mercury News

item.title: Eclipse Theia IDE arrives for desktop, browser
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has announced the release of the Eclipse Theia IDE. Based on the Theia cloud and desktop IDE framework implemented in TypeScript, the Theia IDE is avialable for Windows, Linux, and macOS and can be tried online.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 voice scams to watch out for – and your quick action plan
item.description: Any potential scammer can copy a voice with cheap, accurate AI software in their bid to swindle you out of big money. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Evolving hybrid cloud strategies in modern agencies
item.description: How are the CDC and TSA managing cloud adoption to meet their missions? The post Evolving hybrid cloud strategies in modern agencies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: EU finds Meta's 'pay or consent' advertising model violates tech rules
item.description: The European Union said Meta’s “pay or consent” advertising model for European users violates the bloc’s Digital Markets Act (DMA), according to preliminary findings released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The technology powering Taylor Swift, Netflix and the Sphere
item.description: Disguise is a pioneer in an exploding market for visual graphics in demand from Hollywood to the NBA.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: VMware Disillusionment Drives Customers to Nutanix's Hyperconverged Solutions
item.description: Nutanix, the pioneer of hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI), has emerged as a top choice for customers dissatisfied with VMware's offerings, with its innovative approach and market-leading technology
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Supreme Court keeps on hold efforts in Texas and Florida to regulate social media platforms
item.description: The Florida and Texas laws were signed by Republican governors in the months following decisions by Facebook and Twitter, now X, to cut then-President Donald Trump off over his posts related to the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol by his supporters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Supreme Court Puts Texas and Florida Social Media Laws on Hold
item.description: The Supreme Court has temporarily suspended lower court rulings regarding two state laws aimed at prohibiting social media companies from banning users based on their political views, allowing the major First Amendment challenge to proceed.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: Protect Yourself from Scam Calls: Tracing and Blocking Anonymous Phone Numbers
item.description: Discover how to identify and block hidden IDs on your phone, preventing scam calls and staying one step ahead of unwanted robocalls and telemarketers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How smaller businesses can reimagine brand building with generative AI
item.description: Generating and maintaining awareness is notoriously challenging for small businesses, often working with limited in-house resources and without the budget for external marketing support.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Boeing to Acquire Spirit AeroSystems in $4.7 Billion All-Stock Deal
item.description: In a strategic move, Boeing has announced its plans to acquire Spirit AeroSystems, a leading supplier of aircraft parts and systems, in an all-stock deal worth $4.7 billion, decades after the two companies parted ways.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: EU Regulators Accuse Meta of Violating Digital Competition Rules with Paid Ad-Free Option on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: European Union regulators have accused social media company Meta Platforms of breaching the bloc's new digital competition rulebook by forcing users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them, sparking concerns over consumer choice and fairness.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Robot Boat Revolutionizes Waterway Cleanup with Autonomous Trash Removal
item.description: Discover the Class 3 Clearbot, an innovative autonomous vessel designed to efficiently remove marine trash and plants from waterways, revolutionizing waste management efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 AI-powered apps that’ll actually save you time
item.description: You can’t open your eyes these days without seeing something about generative AI and all the reasons it’s, like, totally gonna revolutionize the way you work. And yet, call me surly, but most of the AI tools out there at this point seem far less impressive in practice than they do on paper.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why (and perhaps how) Apple Intelligence will make money
item.description: We’ll leave it to future retrospectives to show us if it turns out be a good or a bad thing, but artificial intelligence will change everything, so of course Apple is building a business around it. That shouldn’t surprise anyone.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11 Insider Previews: Latest Developments in the New Build
item.description: Microsoft continuously refines the Windows 11 experience, releasing new builds for its insider program, offering a glimpse into upcoming features and enhancements.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: InfoWorld Technology of the Year Awards 2024 Nominations Now Open
item.description: The 2024 InfoWorld Technology of the Year Awards will recognize the best and most innovative products across 26 categories in software development, cloud computing, data analytics, and AI and machine learning. Nominations for the 2024 awards are now open.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 7 steps to improve analytics for data-driven organizations
item.description: When leaders say they want to be a data-driven organization, a key objective is empowering business people to use data, predictive models, generative AI capabilities, and data visualizations to improve decision-making.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Instagram creators could soon make AI versions of themselves
item.description: Think you've seen them? Meta said “AI” will appear in front of the creator’s name followed by a “beta” tag after.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Will China try to make a land grab on the moon?
item.description: “A lot of their so-called civilian space program is a military program. And I think, in effect, we are in a race,” says NASA Administrator Bill Nelson.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Animal rescuers try to keep dozens of dolphins away from Cape Cod shallows after mass stranding
item.description: Animal rescuers were trying to keep dozens of dolphins away from shallow waters around Cape Cod after 125 of the creatures earlier stranded themselves.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Too many passwords to remember? Here's what you can do
item.description: According to some unscientific studies, the average person has hundreds of passwords. That's a lot to keep track of.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Supreme Court nears the end of another momentous term. A decision on Trump's immunity looms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the last 10 days of June, on a frenetic pace of its own making, the Supreme Court touched a wide swath of American society in a torrent of decisions on abortion, guns, the environment, health, the opioid crisis, securities fraud and homelessness.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As AI gains a workplace foothold, states are trying to make sure workers don't get left behind
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — With many jobs expected to eventually rely on generative artificial intelligence, states are trying to help workers beef up their tech skills before they become outdated and get outfoxed by machines that are becoming increasingly smarter.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration
item.description: How will healthcare continue to transform? The post Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Supreme Court Rules in Favor of Biden Administration in Social Media Dispute
item.description: The US Supreme Court has ruled that the federal government can take action against social media companies to combat misinformation about COVID-19, election security and other topics, siding with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A Vatican Radio territory in north Rome once blamed for electro-smog will become a solar farm
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis decreed Wednesday that an area of northern Rome, long the source of controversy because of electromagnetic waves emitted by Vatican Radio towers there, will now house a field of solar panels to fuel Vatican City. Citing the Vatican’s pledge in U.N.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI image-generator startup Stability AI looks for reshoot with new CEO and investor group
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The troubled artificial intelligence company behind image-generator Stable Diffusion is looking for a reshoot with a new CEO and a surge of investment from a group that includes Sean Parker, Facebook’s former president and Napster's co-founder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia won't pay an $8 million ransom after a cyberattack compromised its national data center
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesia's national data center has been compromised by a hacking group asking for a $8 million ransom that the government says it won't pay.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia Refuses to Pay $8 Million Ransom After National Data Center Cyberattack
item.description: Jakarta, Indonesia - A hacking group breached the country's national data center, demanding an $8 million ransom which has been rejected by the government.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
